headline: Maternal Citizenship Amendment Passed in 2024; March 2025 Settlement Closed the Pre-Amendment Gap
context: Parliament passed the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2024 in Oct-Dec 2024 enabling Malaysian mothers' overseas-born children to acquire citizenship. A March 2025 Federal Court consent order covered pre-amendment cases.

cards.text: Maternal citizenship is now in the Constitution, but pre-2024 children needed a separate Federal Court settlement to be covered.
cards.sub: Suriani Kempe v Kerajaan Malaysia ran from 2020 to 2025. The legislative fix arrived in October-December 2024.

cards.h: The Dewan Rakyat passed the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2024 on 17 October 2024; the Dewan Negara followed on 3 December 2024.
cards.s: The amendment enables Malaysian mothers' overseas-born children to acquire citizenship by operation of law from the date of commencement.
cards.d: The Dewan Rakyat passed the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2024 on 17 October 2024; the Dewan Negara followed on 3 December 2024. The amendment enables Malaysian mothers' overseas-born children to acquire citizenship by operation of law from the date of commencement.

cards.h: On 10 March 2025, a five-member Federal Court bench endorsed a consent order between Family Frontiers and the government for pre-amendment cases.
cards.s: Children under 18 born overseas to Malaysian mothers and foreign fathers before the amendment can now apply for citizenship under Article 15(2).
cards.d: On 10 March 2025, a five-member Federal Court bench endorsed a consent order between Family Frontiers and the government for pre-amendment cases. Children under 18 born overseas to Malaysian mothers and foreign fathers before the amendment can now apply for citizenship under Article 15(2).

cards.h: The settlement does not address every gap raised in the campaign, including some categories of stateless children, foundling-status disputes, and adult children.
cards.s: Family Frontiers and other advocacy groups continue to press for residual reforms.
cards.d: The settlement does not address every gap raised in the campaign, including some categories of stateless children, foundling-status disputes, and adult children. Family Frontiers and other advocacy groups continue to press for residual reforms.

cards.text: The story is no longer 'will the government recognise mothers'. It is 'which residual gaps remain after the legislative and judicial fixes settled the main case'.
The amendment + March 2025 settlement closes the central injustice. The remaining work is on the cases the headline reform did not touch.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
